chunk_summary: This section outlines a structured template for advancing enteral nutrition from Day 0 to Day 3 in critically ill pediatric patients, with triggers for initiating supplemental parenteral nutrition based on clinical guidelines and evidence-based practices.
learning_objectives: Understand the Day 0–3 enteral nutrition advancement protocol for critically ill pediatric patients.
Identify clinical triggers for initiating supplemental parenteral nutrition during early nutrition phases.
Analyze evidence-based guidelines for feeding in preterm and critically ill children.
Evaluate the role of early versus late parenteral nutrition in pediatric critical care.
key_facts: Day 0–3 advancement targets are critical for optimizing enteral nutrition in pediatric patients.
ASPEN/SCCM 2017 guidelines emphasize early enteral nutrition initiation in critically ill children.
The PEPaNIC trial highlights risks of delayed parenteral nutrition, including increased infection rates.
ESPNIC recommends monitoring feed tolerance and adjusting nutrition plans accordingly.
Preterm infants require tailored feeding strategies to prevent feed intolerance and ensure growth.
Triggers for supplemental parenteral nutrition include inadequate enteral intake or feed intolerance.
reference_quote: "Early versus late parenteral nutrition in critically ill children (PEPaNIC trial) highlights the importance of timing in reducing infection risks and improving outcomes." (https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa1514762)
document_key_topics: Feeding guidelines for preterm and critically ill children
